mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent, or rolling 3-year IC is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high retail auto sales and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer demand supported by cheap credit, which expands corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS is below 3 percent and RSAFS is in the top quartile, or interaction term IC is negative over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in recovery periods, or the signal generates negative alpha during supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly increases aggregate corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 315.0 indicates persistent inflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 70 percent of periods when CPIAUCSL stays above 315.0, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms synchronized labor and housing market expansion, which boosts consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative forward returns when HOUST declines despite PAYEMS growth, or the interaction effect shows negative IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate earnings guidance and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns in late-cycle periods, or the signal produces positive alpha during manufacturing downturns.
